strategies.name: Application Control
strategies.description: Prevent execution of unapproved or malicious programs including executables, software libraries, scripts, installers, compiled HTML, HTML applications, control panel applets and drivers.

ML1.summary: Application control is implemented on workstations to restrict execution of executables, software libraries, scripts, installers, compiled HTML, HTML applications and control panel applets to an approved set.

ML2.summary: Application control is implemented on internet-facing servers. Allowed and blocked executions are logged. Microsoft's recommended application blocklist is implemented.

ML3.summary: Application control restricts drivers to an approved set. Microsoft's vulnerable driver blocklist is implemented.

strategies.name: Patch Applications
strategies.description: Patch, update or mitigate vulnerabilities in applications and drivers within a risk-appropriate timeframe.

ML1.summary: Patches, updates or vendor mitigations for vulnerabilities in internet-facing services are applied within two weeks. Applications that are no longer supported by vendors are removed.

ML2.summary: A vulnerability scanner is used at least fortnightly to identify missing patches. Patches for vulnerabilities in internet-facing services are applied within 48 hours when exploits exist.

ML3.summary: A vulnerability scanner is used at least weekly. Patches for vulnerabilities in internet-facing services are applied within 48 hours of availability.

strategies.name: Configure Microsoft Office Macro Settings
strategies.description: Configure Microsoft Office macro settings to block macros from the internet and only allow vetted macros in trusted locations or digitally signed with a trusted certificate.

ML1.summary: Microsoft Office macros are disabled for users who do not have a demonstrated business requirement. Macros in files originating from the internet are blocked. Antivirus scanning of macros is enabled.

ML2.summary: Microsoft Office macros are blocked for users that do not have a validated business requirement. Only signed macros are allowed. Safe Attachments scans documents before delivery.

ML3.summary: Only Microsoft Office macros running from within a sandboxed environment, a Trusted Location, or digitally signed by a trusted publisher are allowed to execute.

strategies.name: User Application Hardening
strategies.description: Configure web browsers and Microsoft Office to block or disable support for unneeded features such as Flash, ads, Java, and unnecessary scripting.

ML1.summary: Web browsers are configured to block web-based advertisements. Internet Explorer 11 is disabled or removed. Web browser security settings cannot be changed by users.

ML2.summary: Web browsers do not process Java from the internet. Web content filtering blocks malicious domains. Safe Links rewrites and scans URLs in email and Office documents.

ML3.summary: .NET Framework 3.5 (and earlier) is disabled or removed. PowerShell is configured to use Constrained Language Mode.

strategies.name: Restrict Administrative Privileges
strategies.description: Restrict administrative privileges to operating systems and applications based on user duties. Regularly revalidate the need for privileges.

ML1.summary: Requests for privileged access are validated when first requested. Privileged accounts are not used for reading email, web browsing, or accessing internet services. Legacy authentication is blocked for admin accounts.

ML2.summary: Just-in-time administration is used for administering systems and applications. Privileged access is limited to compliant devices. Admin sessions have enforced sign-in frequency.

ML3.summary: Credential Guard is enabled. Windows Hello for Business or hardware tokens are used for privileged accounts. Administrative activities are centrally logged and audited.

strategies.name: Patch Operating Systems
strategies.description: Patch, update or mitigate vulnerabilities in operating systems within a risk-appropriate timeframe. Remove unsupported operating systems.

ML1.summary: Patches for vulnerabilities in operating systems of internet-facing services are applied within two weeks. Operating systems that are no longer supported by vendors are replaced.

ML2.summary: A vulnerability scanner is used at least fortnightly. Patches for critical vulnerabilities in OS are applied within 48 hours when exploits exist. Automated update rings are configured.

ML3.summary: The latest or previous release of operating systems is used. All host-based firewall policies are enforced.

strategies.name: Multi-Factor Authentication
strategies.description: Multi-factor authentication is used to protect access to data and systems. Phishing-resistant MFA is preferred where possible.

ML1.summary: MFA is used for all users for all internet-facing services. Legacy authentication that cannot use MFA is blocked. Number matching and additional context are enabled for Microsoft Authenticator.

ML2.summary: Phishing-resistant MFA is used for privileged accounts. MFA is required for high and medium risk sign-ins. MFA is required for device registration and enrollment.

ML3.summary: Phishing-resistant MFA is used for all users where possible (FIDO2 or certificate-based). MFA is required for Azure management. Sign-in frequency is enforced. Compliant devices are required.

strategies.name: Regular Backups
strategies.description: Regularly back up important data, software and configuration settings. Retain backups in a secure and resilient manner. Test restoration of backups.

ML1.summary: Backups of important information, software and configuration settings are performed and retained in a coordinated and resilient manner. Audit logs are retained.

ML2.summary: Unprivileged accounts cannot access backups belonging to other accounts. Sensitivity labels protect data classification. External sharing is restricted to existing guests only.

ML3.summary: Unprivileged accounts are prevented from modifying or deleting backups. Sharing defaults to internal only. Anonymous links expire. Downloads are blocked on unmanaged devices.
